Miami FHA Loans

FHA loans in Miami, Florida

Buy a home in Miami with as little as 3.5% down. Here's how FHA loans work in Miami-Dade — including the condo-approval rules that make or break a Miami deal — and how to get pre-approved fast.

Miami Home Loans

FHA loans in Miami, Florida

Miami is one of the most competitive — and condo-heavy — housing markets in the country, which makes FHA financing both popular and a little different here. With 3.5% down and credit from 580, FHA opens the door for first-time buyers and working families across Miami-Dade, from high-rise condos in Brickell and Edgewater to single-family homes in Kendall and Westchester. But because so much of Miami's inventory is condominiums, one detail matters more here than almost anywhere else in Florida: FHA condo approval.

Miami-Dade County FHA limit (1-unit)$667,000

2026 single-family

Minimum down3.5%

With a 580+ score

Min credit score580

500–579 with 10% down

Property types1–4 units

Owner-occupied

What the FHA limit means in Miami

Miami-Dade County is a designated high-cost area, so its 2026 single-family FHA limit is $667,000, above the $541,287 statewide floor — meaning you can finance higher-priced Miami homes than in most of Florida. Confirm Miami-Dade County's exact limit with HUD's official FHA loan-limit lookup. Even with this higher limit, Miami's luxury and waterfront condos often exceed FHA limits — so most FHA buyers here focus on FHA-approved buildings and mid-market homes.

Buying a Miami condo with an FHA loan

Here's the catch that trips up Miami buyers: you can only use an FHA loan on a condo if the building is FHA-approved, or if your specific unit qualifies through FHA Single-Unit Approval. Many Miami condo buildings aren't on HUD's approved list, so it's essential to check approval before you fall for a unit. We verify a building's FHA status up front and steer you toward eligible units — see our FHA condo loans guide. For single-family homes in Miami-Dade, the process is more straightforward.

Down payment help for Miami buyers

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and Miami buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and Miami-Dade County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Neighborhoods we serve in Miami

We help buyers throughout Miami and Miami-Dade County, including Brickell, Edgewater, Little Havana, Coral Way, Kendall, and Westchester. Wherever you're looking, we'll explain your FHA loan limit, rate, and monthly payment — and get you pre-approved fast.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Miami (Miami-Dade County) for 2026?+
Miami-Dade is a designated high-cost county, so its 2026 FHA limit is $667,000, above the $541,287 Florida floor. Confirm the exact current limit with HUD's official FHA loan-limit lookup.
Can I buy a Miami condo with an FHA loan?+
Yes, but the condo building must be FHA-approved, or your unit must qualify through FHA Single-Unit Approval. We check a building's status before you make an offer.
How much down payment do I need to buy in Miami?+
As little as 3.5% with a 580+ credit score. Gift funds are allowed, and sellers can contribute up to 6% toward your closing costs.
Do you offer FHA loans throughout Miami-Dade?+
Yes — we serve buyers across Miami-Dade, including Miami, Hialeah, Doral, Homestead, and Miami Beach.
Are there down payment assistance programs in Miami?+
Yes. Florida Housing programs and Miami-Dade's local SHIP assistance can pair with an FHA loan; funding changes, so we'll help you check current availability.
